explicit_text: I've been involved with the American Federation of Teachers since I started teaching as a part-time instructor. Through AFT and its state federation, AFT Washington, I've engaged in political work around working conditions and helped the union become anti-racist in recent years. I've held various leadership roles including faculty senate president and co-president of the district-wide union local. The union has been a big part of my professional and social life, connecting me with colleagues and supporting my work at Seattle Central College.
implicit_text: I've been involved with the national teachers' union since I started teaching as a part-time instructor. Through this union and its state federation, I've engaged in political work around working conditions and helped the organization become anti-racist in recent years. I've held various leadership roles including faculty senate president and co-president of the district-wide union local. This labor organization has been a big part of my professional and social life, connecting me with colleagues and supporting my work at the community college in Seattle.

explicit_text: I'm active in the Asian Pacific American Labor Alliance, a national multi-union organization representing many Asian and Pacific Islander ethnicities. I serve as a national officer and participate in the Seattle chapter, which has been an important social and political community for me. APALA connects me with friends and colleagues who share similar backgrounds and labor interests, enriching both my social support and activism.
implicit_text: I'm active in a national multi-union organization representing many Asian and Pacific Islander ethnicities. I serve as a national officer and participate in the Seattle chapter, which has been an important social and political community for me. This group connects me with friends and colleagues who share similar backgrounds and labor interests, enriching both my social support and activism.

explicit_text: I've lived on Beacon Hill in Seattle for over 30 years, and it's been my home throughout my teaching career. This neighborhood has been a stable place for me amidst my work and community involvement. Living here has allowed me to stay connected to the city and its diverse communities while working at Seattle Central College nearby.
implicit_text: I've lived on a south Seattle neighborhood for over 30 years, and it's been my home throughout my teaching career. This neighborhood has been a stable place for me amidst my work and community involvement. Living here has allowed me to stay connected to the city and its diverse communities while working at the nearby community college.

explicit_text: Seattle Central College has been my professional home since 1992 when I became a full-time instructor, after eight years as an adjunct. I teach history, American Ethnic Studies, Women's Studies, and International Studies there, and I’m tenured. The college operates on a quarter system and has a large faculty union local. I've been deeply involved in campus life, including organizing events like the Day of Remembrance and advocating for preserving George Tsutakawa's fountain on campus.
implicit_text: This community college in Seattle has been my professional home since 1992 when I became a full-time instructor, after eight years as an adjunct. I teach history, American Ethnic Studies, Women's Studies, and International Studies there, and I’m tenured. The institution operates on a quarter system and has a large faculty union local. I've been deeply involved in campus life, including organizing events like the Day of Remembrance and advocating for preserving a notable fountain on campus created by a renowned sculptor.

explicit_text: In the early 1990s, I taught women's studies at the University of Washington before focusing my career at Seattle Central College. That experience helped shape my academic interests and teaching approach. Although my main work has been at the community college level, my time at UW was an important part of my professional development.
implicit_text: In the early 1990s, I taught women's studies at the flagship public university in Seattle before focusing my career at the local community college. That experience helped shape my academic interests and teaching approach. Although my main work has been at the community college level, my time at the large research university was an important part of my professional development.

explicit_text: George Tsutakawa was an internationally renowned sculptor who attended Broadway Edison High School, the site that later became Seattle Central College. He donated a fountain to the college, which became a cherished piece of artwork on campus. I was involved in a campaign to save and maintain this fountain, connecting my work with the college community and honoring his legacy.
implicit_text: An internationally renowned sculptor attended the high school that later became the community college where I teach. He donated a fountain to the college, which became a cherished piece of artwork on campus. I was involved in a campaign to save and maintain this fountain, connecting my work with the college community and honoring his legacy.

explicit_text: Broadway Edison High School is historically significant because it was the high school George Tsutakawa attended. The school site later became Seattle Central College, where I teach. This connection between the high school and the college adds a layer of historical and cultural meaning to my work, especially in preserving Tsutakawa's fountain on campus.
implicit_text: The historic high school in Seattle where the renowned sculptor attended later became the site of the community college where I teach. This connection between the high school and the college adds a layer of historical and cultural meaning to my work, especially in preserving the sculptor's fountain on campus.

explicit_text: At Seattle Central College, we hold an annual Day of Remembrance to honor the Japanese American incarceration during World War Two. I have been involved in organizing this event, which brings together students, staff, and faculty to reflect on this history. The event also tied into our efforts to preserve the George Tsutakawa fountain, making it a meaningful community activity that connects history, art, and social justice.
implicit_text: At the community college where I teach, we hold an annual event to honor the Japanese American incarceration during World War Two. I have been involved in organizing this event, which brings together students, staff, and faculty to reflect on this history. The event also tied into our efforts to preserve the sculptor's fountain, making it a meaningful community activity that connects history, art, and social justice.

explicit_text: The COVID-19 pandemic abruptly changed my teaching in early 2020, forcing a sudden shift from face-to-face classes to online instruction at Seattle Central College. It was challenging for both students and faculty to adapt, with technology barriers and the emotional toll of isolation. The pandemic blurred work-life boundaries and led to discussions about fair grading policies, as many students struggled to complete courses under difficult circumstances.
implicit_text: The global pandemic abruptly changed my teaching in early 2020, forcing a sudden shift from face-to-face classes to online instruction at the community college where I work. It was challenging for both students and faculty to adapt, with technology barriers and the emotional toll of isolation. The pandemic blurred work-life boundaries and led to discussions about fair grading policies, as many students struggled to complete courses under difficult circumstances.

explicit_text: I live and work in Washington state, specifically in Seattle, where I have been part of the educational and labor communities for decades. During the COVID-19 pandemic, Washington's 'Stay Home, Stay Healthy' order affected how we conducted classes and defined frontline workers. The state's political and social environment has shaped much of my union activism and community involvement.
